flashcards.front: What is an array in data structures?
flashcards.back: An array is a collection of elements of the same data type stored in contiguous memory locations. Each element can be accessed directly using its index.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
flashcards.back: O(1) - constant time complexity because arrays provide direct access to any element using its index.

flashcards.front: What are the two classifications of data structures?
flashcards.back: Primitive data structures (basic types like int, float, char) and Non-primitive data structures (arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs).
A one-dimensional array is a linear collection of elements of the same type, stored in contiguous memory, accessed using a single index.
A two-dimensional array is a collection of elements arranged in rows and columns, like a matrix. It is accessed using two indices - row and column.

flashcards.front: What is row-major order in 2D arrays?
flashcards.back: Row-major order stores array elements row by row - all elements of first row, then second row, and so on. Used in C, C++, and Java.

flashcards.front: What is column-major order in 2D arrays?
flashcards.back: Column-major order stores array elements column by column - all elements of first column, then second column, and so on. Used in Fortran, MATLAB.
A dynamic array is an array that can grow or shrink in size during runtime. It uses automatic resizing when the capacity is exceeded (e.g., ArrayList in Java, vector in C++).
A sparse matrix is a matrix in which most elements are zero. It is represented efficiently using array-based structures to save memory.

flashcards.front: How is memory allocated for arrays in C using malloc?
flashcards.back: Using malloc(size) allocates a block of contiguous memory. For an array of n integers: int *arr = (int*)malloc(n * sizeof(int));

flashcards.front: What is the formula to calculate address of an element in a 1D array?
flashcards.back: Address of element arr[i] = Base Address + (i * size_of_element), where i is the index starting from 0.

flashcards.front: What is the formula to calculate address in row-major 2D array?
flashcards.back: Address of arr[i][j] = Base Address + ((i * n) + j) * size, where n is number of columns.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between array and structure?
flashcards.back: Array contains elements of same data type; structure contains elements of different data types. Array elements are accessed by index; structure members are accessed by name.

flashcards.front: What is a polynomial representation using arrays?
flashcards.back: Polynomials are represented using arrays where the coefficient of each power is stored at the corresponding index. For polynomial P(x) = a0 + a1x + a2x^2, array stores [a0, a1, a2].

flashcards.front: What are the basic operations on arrays?
flashcards.back: Traversal (accessing all elements), Insertion (adding element), Deletion (removing element), Searching (finding element), and Sorting (arranging elements).
